title: sterculia lancaviensis
intro: Sterculia lancaviensis, also known as the 'Kadok' tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is a medium-sized tree native to Sri Lanka and India, valued for its medicinal properties and uses. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Sterculia lancaviensis include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other health issues.
